gaps.title: Payment failure state transition race condition
gaps.breaks: Multiple payment failure events processed simultaneously causing inconsistent subscription state
gaps.scenario: Two payment_failed webhooks arrive at same millisecond for tenant_123's subscription. First sets state to past_due, second processes and incorrectly sets to expired due to stale state check
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent payment_failed webhook events for the same subscription. Verify that only one state transition occurs and the subscription ends up in past_due state, not an inconsistent state.

gaps.title: Webhook replay attack vulnerability
gaps.breaks: Identical webhook events reprocessed causing duplicate subscription state changes
gaps.scenario: Attacker replays a subscription_created webhook for tenant_456, creating duplicate subscriptions or causing billing errors
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates replaying the same Paddle webhook event multiple times. Verify the system detects and rejects duplicate events using idempotency keys and doesn't process the same event more than once.

gaps.title: Tenant isolation leak in webhook processing
gaps.breaks: Webhook handler processes events for wrong tenant due to context propagation failure
gaps.scenario: subscription_updated event for tenant_789 is processed with tenant_123 context, causing tenant_123's subscription data to be modified
gaps.prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in webhook processing. Simulate a webhook event with one tenant ID but processed with a different tenant context. Ensure the operation fails or correctly uses the event's tenant ID.

gaps.title: Partial failure during subscription cancellation
gaps.breaks: Webhook partially processed leaving subscription in inconsistent state
gaps.scenario: subscription_canceled webhook processed, update to subscription table succeeds but update to billing_history table fails, leaving subscription in limbo
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates a partial failure during subscription cancellation processing. Verify the system has proper rollback mechanisms and maintains data consistency across all related tables.

gaps.title: State transition validation bypass
gaps.breaks: Invalid state transitions allowed due to insufficient validation
gaps.scenario: subscription_updated webhook attempts to transition subscription from expired directly to active, bypassing required past_due state
gaps.prompt: Write a test that attempts various invalid state transitions (e.g., expired → active, canceled → active). Verify the system rejects these transitions with appropriate error messages.

gaps.title: Missing webhook event handling
gaps.breaks: Unhandled webhook events cause silent failures
gaps.scenario: Paddle sends a new 'subscription_paused' event that's not in the handler, causing the event to be silently ignored
gaps.prompt: Write a test that simulates an unhandled webhook event type. Verify the system logs an appropriate error and doesn't crash, while ensuring all expected event types are properly handled.

raw_response:
GAPS FOUND: 6

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Payment failure state transition race condition
What breaks: Multiple payment failure events processed simultaneously causing inconsistent subscription state
Real scenario: Two payment_failed webhooks arrive at same millisecond for tenant_123's subscription. First sets state to past_due, second processes and incorrectly sets to expired due to stale state check
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates concurrent payment_failed webhook events for the same subscription. Verify that only one state transition occurs and the subscription ends up in past_due state, not an inconsistent state.

GAP 2
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Webhook replay attack vulnerability
What breaks: Identical webhook events reprocessed causing duplicate subscription state changes
Real scenario: Attacker replays a subscription_created webhook for tenant_456, creating duplicate subscriptions or causing billing errors
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates replaying the same Paddle webhook event multiple times. Verify the system detects and rejects duplicate events using idempotency keys and doesn't process the same event more than once.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Tenant isolation leak in webhook processing
What breaks: Webhook handler processes events for wrong tenant due to context propagation failure
Real scenario: subscription_updated event for tenant_789 is processed with tenant_123 context, causing tenant_123's subscription data to be modified
AI agent prompt: Write a test that verifies tenant isolation in webhook processing. Simulate a webhook event with one tenant ID but processed with a different tenant context. Ensure the operation fails or correctly uses the event's tenant ID.

GAP 4
Severity: HIGH
Title: Partial failure during subscription cancellation
What breaks: Webhook partially processed leaving subscription in inconsistent state
Real scenario: subscription_canceled webhook processed, update to subscription table succeeds but update to billing_history table fails, leaving subscription in limbo
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a partial failure during subscription cancellation processing. Verify the system has proper rollback mechanisms and maintains data consistency across all related tables.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: State transition validation bypass
What breaks: Invalid state transitions allowed due to insufficient validation
Real scenario: subscription_updated webhook attempts to transition subscription from expired directly to active, bypassing required past_due state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that attempts various invalid state transitions (e.g., expired → active, canceled → active). Verify the system rejects these transitions with appropriate error messages.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Missing webhook event handling
What breaks: Unhandled webhook events cause silent failures
Real scenario: Paddle sends a new 'subscription_paused' event that's not in the handler, causing the event to be silently ignored
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates an unhandled webhook event type. Verify the system logs an appropriate error and doesn't crash, while ensuring all expected event types are properly handled.
